perturbation.original_text: The owner and the recipient are bound to disclose confidential information and business data only to those employees who have a need to know this information and business data in order to properly carry out work which is the subject of this agreement and are explicitly warned to be obliged to safe keep the information and business data as confidential and that in case of the violation of confidentiality they are liable for any damage caused.
perturbation.changed_text: The owner and the recipient are bound to disclose confidential information and business data to all employees, regardless of their need to know this information and business data and no warnings are required regarding confidentiality. In case of the violation of confidentiality they are not liable for any damage caused, unless gross negligence can be proven.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that confidential information can be disclosed to *all* employees, regardless of need, and removes the requirement for confidentiality warnings and liability for damages (unless gross negligence is proven). This directly contradicts the original intent of limiting access to only those with a 'need to know' and holding them accountable for breaches. This change impacts other parts of the contract that refer to the protection of confidential information, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties, the own er and the recipient, are bound to guarantee that highly confidential written or verbal INFORMATION or business data which are subjects of this contract shall not be disclosed to third parties. The list of highly confidential INFORMATION includes:  written or verbal information which the owner classifies as confidential,  business discussions during the contract validity term,  documents, letters, meeting minutes and e -mails,  technical documentation, measurement and other reports, calculations and studies, dra fts, photographs, and plans,  lists of suppliers, specifications of material and tools, tools technical,  know -how, inventions and ideas,  promotional and presentational material, price information,  other documentation. The recipient stores the received INFO RMATION and business data systematically, e.g. in files, electronic media and material form. All needed precautions/measures are to be taken to protect the INFORMATION from copying, theft, destruction, damage or any other intrusion. Both parties are bound to take all needed measures to guarantee the protection of data confidentiality. The owner and the recipient are bound to disclose confidential information and business data to all employees, regardless of their need to know this information and business data and no warnings are required regarding confidentiality. In case of the violation of confidentiality they are not liable for any damage caused, unless gross negligence can be proven.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information can be disclosed to all employees, regardless of need, and removes confidentiality warnings and liability, unless gross negligence is proven. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which binds both parties to guarantee that confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties and requires precautions to protect the information.

perturbation.original_text: In case the recipient, with the prior written consent of the owner, engages sub -suppliers to carry out the subject of this agreement, a written confidentiality agreement is to be presented to the owner, which has been signed between the re cipient and a sub -supplier. The contents of the agreement shall be identical to this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In case the recipient engages sub -suppliers, no prior written consent of the owner is necessary, and a confidentiality agreement is optional and can be different from this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for the owner's consent for sub-suppliers and making the confidentiality agreement optional, potentially with different terms. This undermines the initial agreement about data protection, which creates uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality with the sub-suppliers. This directly contradicts the obligation for sub-suppliers to adhere to the same confidentiality terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case the recipient engages sub -suppliers, no prior written consent of the owner is necessary, and a confidentiality agreement is optional and can be different from this agreement. The confidential information is allowed to be communicated to third parties only with the express written consent of the owner. If the owner’s confidential inf ormation is disclosed by the recipient, it shall remain the exclusive property of the owner, but the recipient is liable to any damage caused. For the term of 10 years, after receiving the INFORMATION and business data, the confidential information shall be kept safely, unless defined differently by a special agreement or it is proved to have been made publicly known. The obligation does not terminate even if one of the parties backs out of the contract, e.g. due to resigning from the cooperation or from t he planned project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that no prior written consent of the owner is necessary for engaging sub-suppliers, and the confidentiality agreement is optional and can be different from the original agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the recipient's obligations regarding confidential information disclosed to sub-suppliers, implying that these obligations exist regardless of owner consent or the terms of a potentially different confidentiality agreement. The changed text undermines the enforceability of confidentiality with sub-suppliers, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the handling of confidential information as per the provided explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The confidential information is allowed to be communicated to third parties only with the express written consent of the owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information can be communicated to third parties at any time without the owner's consent.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct contradiction by stating that confidential information can be disclosed to third parties at any time without the owner's consent, which is opposite from the clause specifying it can be communicated only with explicit consent. This change creates legal uncertainty and defeats the purpose of the entire non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case the recipient engages sub -suppliers, no prior written consent of the owner is necessary, and a confidentiality agreement is optional and can be different from this agreement. The confidential information can be communicated to third parties at any time without the owner's consent. If the owner’s confidential inf ormation is disclosed by the recipient, it shall remain the exclusive property of the owner, but the recipient is liable to any damage caused. For the term of 10 years, after receiving the INFORMATION and business data, the confidential information shall be kept safely, unless defined differently by a special agreement or it is proved to have been made publicly known. The obligation does not terminate even if one of the parties backs out of the contract, e.g. due to resigning from the cooperation or from t he planned project.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidential information can only be communicated to third parties with express written consent. The changed text states that the confidential information can be communicated to third parties at any time without the owner's consent. This creates a direct contradiction with the original clause. The contradicted text contains the same clause introduced by the changed text (The confidential information can be communicated to third parties at any time without the owner's consent.), which directly opposes the initial requirement of express written consent as stated in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: For the term of 10 years, after receiving the INFORMATION and business data, the confidential information shall be kept safely, unless defined differently by a special agreement or it is proved to have been made publicly known.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information shall be kept safely for a term of 1 year, after which it can be disclosed. No special agreement can change this.
perturbation.explanation: The modified clause drastically shortens the term for which information must be kept confidential from 10 years to 1 year, and forbids special agreements from superseding the 1 year limitation. This directly conflicts with the original clause and adds an inflexible end date, creating potential disputes over the duration of confidentiality obligations and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information can be communicated to third parties at any time without the owner's consent. If the owner’s confidential inf ormation is disclosed by the recipient, it shall remain the exclusive property of the owner, but the recipient is liable to any damage caused. The confidential information shall be kept safely for a term of 1 year, after which it can be disclosed. No special agreement can change this. The obligation does not terminate even if one of the parties backs out of the contract, e.g. due to resigning from the cooperation or from t he planned project. In this case, the recipient is to return all the material file of the INFORMATION and business data at once or at the latest in 7 (seven) days after receiving the request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality term from 10 years to 1 year and forbids special agreements from altering this, which contradicts the original allowance for differing terms via special agreements. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text because it also explicitly sets the term to 1 year and prohibits special agreements from changing it. Even though the Changed Text and Contradicted text are at the same location, the Perturbation Explanation states that the Changed Text modifies the original clause and adds an inflexible end date. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In this case, the recipient is to return all the material file of the INFORMATION and business data at once or at the latest in 7 (seven) days after receiving the request.
perturbation.changed_text: In this case, the recipient is not required to return any material file of the INFORMATION and business data, and may retain it indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the clause requiring the return of confidential information upon request. It creates a direct conflict by stating the recipient is *not* required to return any material and can retain it indefinitely. This severely weakens the owner's control over their confidential data and creates significant uncertainty regarding data security upon contract termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information shall be kept safely for a term of 1 year, after which it can be disclosed. No special agreement can change this. The obligation does not terminate even if one of the parties backs out of the contract, e.g. due to resigning from the cooperation or from t he planned project. In this case, the recipient is not required to return any material file of the INFORMATION and business data, and may retain it indefinitely. The recipient is to return also those INFORMATION and business d ata which were given to the eventual sub -supplier.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the recipient is *not* required to return any material file and can retain it indefinitely. This directly contradicts the original text which requires the recipient to return all material within 7 days of receiving a request. Further, the contradicted text includes both the requirement for returning information after a year and the negative constraint created by the change: 'In this case, the recipient is not required to return any material file of the INFORMATION and business data, and may retain it indefinitely.' This creates a clear and undeniable contradiction within the document according to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: 'Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: The agreement is valid when signed by both parties of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is valid only when notarized and registered with the local municipality, and is subject to annual review and renewal.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by changing the conditions for the agreement's validity. The original text states it's valid upon signing by both parties, but the modification adds the requirement of notarization, registration, and annual review/renewal. This introduces uncertainty and could lead to disputes over whether the agreement is actually in effect at any given time, especially if these new conditions are not met.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The changes and additional clauses to this agreement are only allowed in writ ten form. This agreement is audited and carried out in accordance with the Law of the Republic of Slovenia. The rights and obligations which are not defined by this agreement, are defined by “The General Purchasing Conditions” of Domel and the Obliga tion Code (The Official Gazette of the Republic of Slovenia, no. 83/2001). This agreement is valid only when notarized and registered with the local municipality, and is subject to annual review and renewal. Both parties agree that eventual disputes arising from this contract are to be solved in agreement. If this i s not possible, the Court of Law related to the owner, i.e. Domel, is competent to deal with the dispute.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces new conditions for the agreement's validity (notarization, registration, annual review/renewal) that contradict the original condition of validity upon signing by both parties. The contradicted text repeats the conditions in the changed text. Since the modified text explicitly states different requirements than what was originally required for the agreement to be valid, and now those changed requirements are within the contradicted text itself, a strong contradiction exists, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, causing uncertainty and potential disputes about the agreement's effective date.
